title: veronica bellidioides subsp. bellidioides
intro: Veronica bellidioides subsp. bellidioides, commonly known as bell heather or bell foxglove, is a flowering plant species in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and asthma due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Veronica bellidioides subsp. bellidioides include reducing fever, relieving congestion, and soothing sore throats, making it a popular herbal remedy for respiratory conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be prepared by steeping the leaves in hot water or mixing them with a solvent to create a concentrated extract.
